Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And For Modification Of The Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust Dated August 15, 1992 (Probate Code Sections 15404, 15409 And 17200(B)(13))
Off Calendar Jun-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-89-201334 Estate of Louis R. Lurie & PTR-13-296844 The Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust (RALFIT).

Appearances: Daniel Rapaport & Virginia Palmer for Dana Lurie who is present. Ernest Der for (successor trustee) Debra Dolch who is present. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo. Ali Gramaglia for Robert A. Lurie who is present. David Jolivet for William Fay, Hannah Lurie, Jack Lurie, Robert L. Fay, Olivia R. Fay, Juliette Weis & Milo Black. Edward Koplowitz & Andrew Timkin for Robert Lurie, Betsy Weis, Ann Black & Patricia Lurie. Lawrence Siracusa, Guardian ad Litem for the unborn children of the adopted children. Monica Dell'Osso (via court call) Guardian ad Litem for the unborn issue of the biological children of Robert Lurie. Brian Rocca (via court call) for Robert A. Lurie.

The court stated that we are here for the petition for approval of settlement and modification of the trust, that it is pleased to see all of the parties here and able to reach a settlement, that it has reviewed the paperwork, that it appears appropriate but there are some issues. The court addressed the proposed order indicating that separate orders are needed for the two cases, requested that the signature page be modified to include some text, that some of the findings are not appropriate to be in the order and requested that the revised order be emailed to her in the complex litigation department. Mr. Koplowitz stated that the settlement agreement and the proposed order have been revised extensively, that administration of the trust will go on indefinitely and that historical recitation makes sense to that years from now it will be helpful. The court agreed that it could be left in, stated that there was no bond requirement for the successor trustee, and that per page 6 the biological children could appoint a successor trustee. Mr. Koplowitz stated that it was on calendar for 7/1/15. The court stated that issue regarding bond may come up on 7/1/15, that language in the order regarding jurisdiction was not in the settlement agreement and that the clients must be here to make the change. Mr. Rapaport stated that it was envisioned after the approval and responded to the question regarding the ages of Dana (47) and David (45). The court asked if there was a spendthrift provision, stated that the subtrust is to be terminated at age 50, confirmed that the implications have been discussed with the clients and asked who will be paying the Guardian ad Litems. Mr. Siracusa stated that he will be filing for fees and discharge and understood his fees would be paid out of RALFIT. Ms. Dell'Osso stated that she is not sure who the source of payment of her fees is. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he expected they will come from the remaining 2 trusts. Mr. Rocca stated that they need to check whether it is appropriate to pay her fees from RALFIT or the remaining trusts. Mr. Koplowitz expressed concern regarding the petitions on calendar for 7/1/15, that people will be flying in from New York, the need to get the draft out and the order signed quickly and that interlineations on the orders sometimes create issues. The court addressed the issue of bond regarding the 7/1/15 hearing and that per local rule it is required on successor trustees unless waived. Mr. Der stated that an bond in the amount of $11,000,000.00 exists on the RALFIT and can remain in place. The court stated that once the subtrusts are created Dana & David can waive bond and reminded counsel to update appearances for today on the order, add text to the signature page and to email an order for each case to her. The Court granted the petition for approval of settlement agreement and modification of the Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust (both cases)(subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And Confirmation Of Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Approval Of Accounting And Payment Of Trustee'S Fees
Off Calendar Aug-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-89-201334 The Estate of Robert R. Lurie and PTR-13-296844 The Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust ("RALFIT").

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Robert L. Lurie, Betsy Weis, Patricia Lurie and Anne Black. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank (Trustee). Bette Epstein for James Hunt (Co-Trustee only in the Estate of Louis R. Lurie). Daniel Rapaport for David Lurie and Dana Lurie. Brian Rocca for and with Robert A. Lurie (Trustee). David Baer for William Fay and Olivia Fay.

The Parties agreed that nothing is remaining on the RALFIT (296844) matter. The Parties reported that Judge Garcia (Discovery Referee) has heard one dispute, that one is pending, that briefing is due in the next couple of weeks and that there are a series of meetings that Judge Ross is organizing. Ms. Epstein stated that a meeting was scheduled with Mr. Hunt but that he is currently ill. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the matter is ready for trial. The Court held discussion with the Parties regarding availability of trial and asked the Parties to discuss a Monday trial date for setting in Department 206. The Parties were unable to agree on a trial date. The Court stated that is inclined to set a trial date of December 8, 2014 but that it has some concerns about it. The Court encouraged the Parties to try and target a December trial date. All matters in case number 201334 were continued to September 16, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status to see where the Parties stand regarding documentary evidence.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Wendy Wachhorst, CSR#12290 (650) 208-0405. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chin. (Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And Confirmation Of Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Approval Of Accounting And Payment Of Trustee'S Fees
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Continued To Aug-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-89-201334 Estate of Louis R. Lurie and PTR-13-296844 The Robert A. Lurie Family Irrevocable Trust ("RALFIT").

Appearances: Lisa Kobialka for Robert L. Lurie (Trust matter). Edward Koplowitz for Robert L. Lurie, Patricia Lurie (present), Betsy Weis and Anne Black (both matters). David Baer for William Faye and other Great-grandchildren (both matters). Thomas Worth for James Hunt (Trust matter). Donn Pickett for Robert A. Lurie (both matters). Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank (Estate matter). Bette Epstein for James Hunt (Estate matter). Daniel Rapaport for Dana Lurie (present) and David Lurie (both matters).

Mr. Worth and Mr. Koplowitz presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether the accountings are complete/incomplete through 2013, whether the accountings were filed timely, fees/charges for preparation of the accountings, Trustee fees, whether these fees were disclosed, whether Mr. Hunt acted in good faith, etc. Discussion was held regarding how the accountings were broken down, Trust language, Trust provisions, appointment of Judge Garcia for discovery issues, whether the Trustees are doing anything different from what they have been doing for the last 20 years, whether the Trustees are incorrectly interpreting the Trust, Louis Lurie's intent, whether or not there is any forensic evidence, whether to set a trial date, whether discovery should be conducted prior to setting a trial date, the Court's stay order, the stipulations, the anti-slapp motion, the issue of whether Mr. Rapport's Clients are beneficiaries/entitled to distributions, etc. The accountings were taken under submission. All other matters were continued to August 19, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 at which time the matters may be set for trial.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Connie Parchman, CSR#6137 (510) 853-5260. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Stewart. (Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Amendment To Former Trustee'S Account
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with Line #57, PES-89-201334, Estate of Louis R. Lurie.

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for and with Robert L. Lurie. Daniel Rapaport for Dana Lurie and David Lurie. Brian Rocca and Donn Pickett for Robert A. Lurie. Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank. David Baer for William Fay, Olivia Fay, and Robert Fay. Bette Epstein for James Hunt.

Mr. Baer presented a proposed order on the ex parte petition for appointment of Guardian Ad Litem. No objections to the ex parte petition were made. Mr. Rocca reported that progress has been made since receiving new discovery, that the parties will meet and confer about the necessity of a discovery referee and that Judge Garcia has agreed to act as a discovery referee. The Court instructed counsel to define the case numbers and the parameters. Mr. Rocca will submit a proposed stipulation and order approved as to form.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that he will be filing a motion for discovery on the motions to strike by April 9, 2014. Opposition papers are due by April 21, 2014. Reply papers are due by April 25, 2014 and the hearing on said motion is set for April 30, 2014 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The underlying petitions are continued to that same date (April 30, 2014 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204). The hearings on the motions to strike currently set for April 23, 2014 are continued to May 21, 2014 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Court recommends the parties consider mediation. Status on filing of the amendment to Former Trustee's account is off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. Not Reported.

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And Confirmation Of Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Approval Of Accounting And Payment Of Trustee'S Fees
Off Calendar Feb-28-2014 Continued To May-14-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz and Lisa Kobialka for and with Robert L. Lurie. Thomas Worth for James Hunt (Trustee). Daniel Rapaport for Dana Lurie and David Lurie. Brian Rocca for Robert A. Lurie. Jonna Thomas for Olivia Fay, Robert L. Fay and William Fay (Remainderman Great Grandchildren). Andrew Zabronsky for Wells Fargo Bank.

Ms. Thomas stated that she will be filing a Petition for Appointment of Guardian Ad Litem of the minor Grandchildren. Mr. Worth informed the Court that Debra Dolch has been appointed as Successor Trustee. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the accounting, whether the accounting can be settled, the Trustee's discretion/powers, amounts of distribution some of the Beneficiaries received, Trustee's fees, whether the Motion for Protective Order should be heard before the Case Management Conference or at the same time, etc. The Parties agreed to the Court ruling on the accounting for 2012/2013 and to the extent of the objections raised on the prior accountings. The Motion for Protective Order (case number 201334) was continued to February 21, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The CMC (case number 201334) is also set for that same date and time. The accounting was taken under submission. Mr. Worth is to prepare and circulate a proposed order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Bond
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Continued To Jan-28-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Trustee). Edward A. Koplowitz for Robert (Bobby) Lurie.

The Court indicated that the Order is on its desk and will get to it soon. The status on filing of bond was continued to January 28, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Not Reported.

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And Confirmation Of Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Approval Of Accounting And Payment Of Trustee'S Fees
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Continued To Jan-28-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz and Lisa Kobialka for Robert L. Lurie. Daniel Rapaport for and with Dana Lurie and for David Lurie. Donn Pickett for and with Robert A. Lurie (Trustor). Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch (proposed Successor-Trustee). thomas Worth for and with James Hunt (Trustee). Particia Lurie also present.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that both sides are agreeable to Ms. Dolch serving as Successor-Trustee. Discussion was held with regard to Mr. Hunt's resignation, Mr. Koplowitz' request for removal of Mr. Hunt, the Court reviewing/approving Mr. Hunt's accounting for a twenty year period (1992 through 2012), Mr. Hunt supplementing the 2013 accounting to run through December 2013, etc. The Court will accept Mr. Hunt's resignation effective December 31, 2013 and Ms. Dolch being appointed at the same time. Bond in the amount of $11,000,000.00 is required for Ms. Dolch. Status on filing of bond is set for January 14, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Trustee having tendered his resignation, the Petition for Removal is moot. Mr. Worth is to prepare an order. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Trustee fees. The accounting and the issue of Trustee fees was continued to January 28, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The hearings on calendar for February 18, 2014 (Petition for Instructions) and February 25, 2014 (Petition for Declaratory Relief, Preliminary and Permanent Injunction, etc.) in case number 201334 The Lurie Foundation Trust are advanced to January 28, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton. (Not Reported)

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And Confirmation Of Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Approval Of Accounting And Payment Of Trustee'S Fees
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Continued To Nov-12-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee James L. Hunt And To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Continued To Nov-12-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz and Lisa Kobialka for Robert (Bobbie) L. Lurie. Donn Pickett for and with Robert A. Lurie (Settlor). Thomas Worth for James Hunt (Mr. Hunt also appeared in Propria Persona). Other Parties that were present in Court: Andrew Zabronsky, Susan Briggs, David Bradlow and Joseph Smith.

The Court made reference to the Foundation Trust case number 201334, the hearing dates of November 21, 2013 and January 28, 2014 in said matter, and stated that all matters should be heard together at some point. Mr. Koplowitz outlined the four issues that are before the Court: 1) termination of Mr. Hunts trusteeship 2) identity and the process of selection of a Successor Trustee 3) presentation and review of Mr. Hunt's accounting and 4) Mr. Hunt's request for Trustee fees. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the process by which to select a Successor Trustee, problems that may arise if the Successor Trustees are rejected, enforcement of provision 2.4 of the Trust (independent selection of a Trustee), whether the Beneficiaries should have some say as to the selection of the Successor Trustee, the minority Beneficiaries interest, qualifications of Mr. Bradlow to serve as Successor Trustee, selection of a neutral Fiduciary, etc. The Court encouraged Counsel to work together to find a solution, stated that it is not troubled by the change in firm name, that it is not accepting resignation of Mr. Hunt today and suggested Bingham McCutchen propose some names. The Court also stated that the accounting petition needs to be amended to include the request for fees and that Mr. Koplowitz needs file his objections. The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. and the Petition for Acceptance of Resignation of Trustee, etc. were continued to November 12, 2013 at 10:30 a.m. (special set), in Dept. 204 to see if an amended petition is on file and where the Parties stand on selecting a Trustee.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Clark. (Not Reported)
